summary: Backend-only testing for ManuscriptForge. 23/23 pytest cases passed including health, JWT auth (register/login/me/logout), Google session error handling, manuscripts CRUD (with cross-user isolation), section generation for 'abstract' via Claude Sonnet 4.5 (>500 chars, persisted), invalid section key (400), Crossref reference search, and exports for md/docx/pdf plus invalid format (400).

minor.issue: N+1 query: re-fetches each manuscript with sections to compute generated_sections count. Use Mongo aggregation or include sections in initial projection.

Returns 200 even when called without any token / with a JWT token (JWT is stateless and not invalidated). Acceptable but could mislead callers — consider documenting or returning a different response when no session was deleted.
On upstream non-200, raises 401 with detail 'Invalid Google session' — fine. But session_token is stored as plaintext (matches Emergent pattern). Acceptable.

action_items: (Optional/Low) Optimize GET /api/manuscripts listing to avoid per-item extra Mongo query.
(Optional) Add rate-limiting / size guards around section generation endpoint (currently no protection against repeated long LLM calls).
(Optional) Consider returning manuscript_id immediately and streaming section generation via SSE/WebSocket — current sync call can hold the connection 30-90s.
critical_code_review_comments: JWT_SECRET in /app/backend/.env still contains the literal 'change-in-production' string — fine for preview, must be rotated for prod.
server.py is ~810 lines; consider splitting into modules (auth.py, manuscripts.py, generation.py, references.py, export.py) per the >700 LOC guideline.
Section generation endpoint is fully synchronous; for 20 sections × ~60s this is brittle. A background-job / status-polling pattern would scale better.
CORS_ORIGINS defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers will reject credentialed requests with wildcard origin. Either restrict origins or set allow_credentials=False.
_user_from_session_token compares datetime strings — works because ISO-8601 is monotone, but consider storing native datetimes for clarity.
All Mongo reads correctly exclude _id ({_id: 0}) — good.
Pydantic validation, HTTP status codes (400/401/404/502), and env-driven config are all clean.
test_credentials: demo@manuscriptforge.app / Demo123! (verified working). /app/memory/test_credentials.md is currently empty — main agent should populate it.
seed_data_creation: Tests create and clean up TEST_-prefixed manuscripts. They also register ephemeral users (test_<uuid>@..., other_<uuid>@...) which are NOT cleaned up — left in DB intentionally for re-run isolation. Demo manuscript test data is deleted at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py as the regression suite. Demo user: demo@manuscriptforge.app / Demo123!. The 'test_generate_abstract' case hits the real Emergent LLM (Claude Sonnet 4.5) and took ~10s in this run but may take 30-90s. JUnit XML at /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in backend. All 16 requested feature tests passed.
